Title: Petronas buyout of PPC stake seen lifting PetChem's earnings
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd (PetChem) could benefit majorly if its parent, Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas), emerges as the buyer of its 50 per cent stake in Pengerang Petrochemical Company Sdn Bhd (PPC), a move analysts say could remove a major earnings drag while unlocking value for shareholders.
Hong Leong Investment Bank (HLIB) said the potential divestment, which is expected to be completed by the end of 2026, would mark a key catalyst for the integrated chemicals producer, improving profitability by eliminating losses from its investment in PPC.
The proposal follows Saudi Aramco's agreement in May to sell its entire 50 per cent stakes in PPC and Pengerang Refining Company Sdn Bhd to Petronas as part of a broader restructuring of the Pengerang Integrated Complex, although financial terms were not disclosed. Under the original 2017 agreement, Aramco had invested about US$7 billion for its 50 per cent stake in the two companies.
While Petronas, which owns a 64.4 per cent stake in PetChem, has yet to confirm whether it will also acquire PetChem's interest in PPC, HLIB believes such a move would offer compelling strategic and financial benefits.
HLIB analyst Thye May Ting said full ownership of PPC would allow Petronas to integrate upstream refining and downstream petrochemical operations at Pengerang under a single entity.
"Should Petronas ultimately acquire full control of PPC, the strategic rationale would be to integrate upstream and downstream operations at Pengerang under a single entity, enabling better operational management, enhanced energy security and full control over the integrated complex," he said in a note.
For PetChem, the disposal would remove one of its largest earnings drags, strengthen profitability and potentially pave the way for greater shareholder value creation.
PetChem returned to profitability in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, posting a net profit of RM401 million after recording losses for four consecutive quarters. However, quarterly revenue declined 8.0 per cent year-on-year to RM7.02 billion.
From the company's perspective, exiting the venture would eliminate a sizeable earnings burden. PPC is currently estimated to generate annual losses of between RM500 million and RM600 million attributable to PetChem's 50 per cent share.
"We view this as a near-term catalyst, as a successful disposal would remove significant earnings drag and reduce the group's forex exposure," Thye said following a recent engagement with PetChem chief financial officer Mohd Azli Ishak and the company's investor relations team.
Besides strengthening earnings, the disposal could also improve PetChem's balance sheet and potentially provide greater financial flexibility.
Investors are also increasingly focused on whether the transaction could pave the way for a special dividend.
However, Thye noted that any capital distribution would depend on the final disposal value and the structure of the transaction.
"We understand that the proposed disposal would require minority shareholders' approval and is likely contingent on the completion of the ongoing Aramco transaction with Petronas," he said.
To safeguard shareholder interests, any transaction would also require an independent valuation and advice from an independent financial adviser to ensure it is conducted on an arm's-length, willing-buyer-willing-seller basis, he said.
Thye also noted that retaining the marketing rights for petrochemical products produced at the Pengerang complex remains an important consideration for PetChem, even if it exits its equity ownership in PPC.
"Nevertheless, we remain cautious that selling prices of key petrochemical products are likely to peak in FY26 before moderating in FY27, assuming the ceasefire agreement holds and supply normalises," he said.
Operationally, the research house said PPC resumed operations about a week ago after being shut down at the end of March due to feedstock constraints. The facility is currently operating at around 50 per cent utilisation as production gradually ramps up.
PetChem, one of Southeast Asia's leading integrated chemicals producers, has a diversified portfolio covering olefins, polymers, fertilisers, methanol and a wide range of petrochemical products. Its largest shareholders are Petronas with a 64.4 per cent stake, followed by the Employees Provident Fund (13.4 per cent) and Amanah Saham Nasional Bhd (4.2 per cent).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Weststar-Rocket Technologies targets over RM42bil in aerospace, defence opportunities
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Weststar Group and Australia's Rocket Technologies International Pty Ltd (RTI) have formed a strategic joint venture (JV) expected to unlock commercial opportunities exceeding RM42 billion through future aerospace, defence, satellite and advanced technology programmes in Malaysia and Australia.
The partnership, through Weststar Defence Industries Sdn Bhd, will see the two companies establish JV entities in Malaysia and Australia to develop sovereign aerospace and defence capabilities, deepening bilateral cooperation in advanced technologies while supporting long-term industrial and economic growth.
The JV agreement (JVA) was signed by The Weststar Group founder and group managing director Tan Sri Dr Syed Azman Syed Ibrahim and RTI founder Allan James Payne at the National Convention Centre in Canberra.
The signing was witnessed by Tan Sri Dr Johari Abdul, Speaker of Malaysia's Dewan Rakyat; Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin, Malaysia's Defence Minister; and Datin Paduka Sharrina Abdullah, Malaysia's High Commissioner to Australia.
Under the agreement, the parties will focus on the manufacturing and assembly of integrated defence systems, civilian satellite platforms and specialised government satellite solutions with local design and content.
They will also provide rocket testing, certification and maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) services, while developing orbital and sub-orbital launch capabilities.
In addition, the partnership will support workforce development through technical education, industry training and research and development partnerships with leading aerospace and defence universities in Malaysia and Australia.
Syed Azman described the agreement as a major milestone in strengthening sovereign industrial capabilities and strategic technology cooperation between the two countries.
"This Strategic Joint Venture Agreement represents more than a business partnership. It reflects a long-term commitment towards building sovereign aerospace and defence capabilities, creating high-value industries and strengthening technological collaboration between Malaysia and Australia.
"We believe this collaboration will create meaningful opportunities for industry growth, talent development, innovation and economic value creation for both nations while contributing towards regional capability enhancement and long-term strategic cooperation," he said.
Payne said the partnership marks an important step in advancing regional aerospace and defence innovation through trusted international collaboration.
"This partnership combines the industrial strengths, expertise and long-term vision of both organisations to develop advanced sovereign capabilities that will support future industry growth, technology advancement and regional cooperation between Australia and Malaysia," he said.
The JVA aligns with the growing momentum of the Malaysia-Australia Comprehensive Strategic Partnership, particularly in defence industry cooperation, advanced technologies, innovation, education and economic development.
The collaboration is also expected to create new opportunities for local industry participation, high-skilled employment, research commercialisation and the development of sustainable aerospace and defence ecosystems in both countries.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: More than 1,000 schools nationwide deemed structurally unsafe
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: More than 1,000 schools nationwide have been deemed structurally unsafe, with the Education Ministry continuing efforts to replace the buildings in phases.
Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said that as of the first quarter of 2026, a total of 1,061 schools had buildings categorised under Scale 7, the classification used for dilapidated structures.
Scale 7 refers to buildings certified as unsafe by the Public Works Department.
Fadhlina said the figure was dynamic and could change over time as assessments and upgrading works progressed.
"The Education Ministry remains committed to providing safe and conducive educational infrastructure for students and school communities nationwide," she said in a written reply.
Datuk Willie Anak Mongin (GPS-Puncak Borneo) had asked for the number of dilapidated schools by state, teacher-to-student ratios in urban and rural areas, and teacher vacancies by state.
Fadhlina said state education departments continuously assessed the need and feasibility of constructing replacement school buildings for schools categorised under Scale 7.
Applications for replacement projects that meet the required criteria would be submitted in stages to the relevant central agencies for consideration under the national development planning framework.
On teacher distribution, she said the ministry considered several factors when determining teacher placements, including student enrolment, whether schools were located in urban or rural areas, subject requirements, teachers' areas of specialisation, and existing vacancies.
She said the approach was aimed at ensuring a more balanced distribution of teachers based on the actual needs of schools."
As of June, the ministry has 422,804 teachers in service, representing a staffing rate of 98.11 per cent," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Microsoft to cut under 2.5pct of workforce in latest layoffs, Business Insider reports
Article:
BENGALURU: Microsoft is planning to cut fewer than 2.5 per cent of its workforce in its latest round of layoffs, which could be announced as early as next week, Business Insider reported on Tuesday, citing sources.
Reuters could not immediately verify the report.
US companies have continued to trim headcount in recent months, with a fresh wave of layoffs across the technology, media and finance sectors as firms rein in costs while investing heavily in AI infrastructure.
Here are a few details:
* The layoffs will affect thousands of roles, including positions in sales and consulting, as well as jobs in the Xbox gaming division, the *Business Insider* report said.
* Microsoft declined to comment on the report.
* Microsoft had roughly 228,000 full-time employees as of **June 30, 2025**, according to an SEC filing last year.
* Xbox, which raised prices of its gaming consoles worldwide, citing a deepening global components crisis, is planning major layoffs and significant cuts to marketing and other budgets, *Bloomberg News* reported earlier this month.
* The Windows maker is also considering options for its Xbox gaming unit, including a potential spin-off or restructuring into a wholly owned subsidiary, *The Information* reported earlier in June.
* In **July 2025**, the company said it would lay off nearly 4 per cent of its workforce in one of its largest rounds of layoffs in recent years.
* Across the technology sector, Meta announced plans this year to cut 10 per cent of its workforce, while Amazon outlined plans to eliminate roughly 16,000 jobs globally.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Data centre power demand in Peninsular Malaysia to surge to 31pct by 2035
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Electricity consumption by data centres in Peninsular Malaysia is projected to surge from seven per cent of total demand in 2026 to 31 per cent by 2035, Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said.
In a written parliamentary reply, he said electricity use by the data centre sector is expected to increase from 10,544 gigawatt-hours (GWh) in 2026 to 73,274 GWh by 2035, based on projections by the Planning and Implementation of Electricity Supply and Tariff Committee.
He said the government, through the Energy Commission, was closely monitoring rising electricity demand, particularly in Peninsular Malaysia, to ensure the country's power supply remains secure and reliable.
Fadillah said the rapid growth of artificial intelligence (AI) and data centre industries would also drive a sharp increase in peak electricity demand.
"Peak demand in Peninsular Malaysia is projected to grow at a compound annual growth rate of 5.1 per cent between 2026 and 2035, rising from 21.3 gigawatts (GW) in 2026 to 33.5GW by 2035," he said.
Fadillah was responding to Chong Zhemin (PH-Kampar), who asked about the country's readiness to meet rising electricity demand driven by data centres and the AI industry.
To ensure electricity supply security, he said the government and the Energy Commission were implementing several measures, including extending the operation of existing thermal power plants as a short-term solution.
He said the commission was also carrying out a request for proposal exercise to add new gas-fired generation capacity to strengthen Peninsular Malaysia's baseload electricity supply.
Fadillah said data centre operators could also procure renewable energy directly from private developers under the Corporate Renewable Energy Supply Scheme through the Third-Party Access mechanism.
He added that the government was accelerating the development of Large Scale Solar projects, targeting a combined renewable energy generation capacity of 4GW for the Peninsular Malaysia grid.
At the same time, he said the government was encouraging data centre operators to generate their own electricity based on their operational needs instead of relying entirely on the national grid.
"This approach will reduce dependence on the grid while helping to ensure the continued stability of the electricity supply system," he said.
Fadillah also said the government, through the Data Centre Task Force, continued to apply stringent screening to new data centre applications and proposals to expand existing facilities.
He said approvals would only be granted if proposed projects matched local grid capacity planning.
"The government will not compromise on the safety and security of electricity supply for the people while supporting the sustainable, competitive and investor-friendly development of Malaysia's data centre ecosystem," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Missing hiker found safe after three days in ravine
Article:
YAN: Three hikers saved a 34-year-old man who had been missing for three days after they heard his cries for help from a 20m-deep ravine at Padang Tok Sheikh in Gunung Jerai last night.
The man had been missing since Sunday after he went hiking alone at 4pm and slipped into the ravine.
The Kedah Fire and Rescue Department operations centre said it received a distress call at 7.30pm.
A team of nine personnel from the Guar Chempedak Fire and Rescue Station, led by Senior Fire Officer I Mohd Shahril Abdul Rahman, was deployed to the scene.
Senior Operations Commander Deputy Fire Superintendent II Ahmad Naufal Abdullah also arrived shortly after.
The operations commander said they met the three hikers at the scene, who reported hearing the victim shouting for help from the ravine, about 15m to 20m from the edge.
Based on public information, the victim had been missing for nearly three days. Initial attempts by rescuers to call out to him from the edge went unanswered.
Firefighters then set up a rope system to descend into the ravine to locate the man.
The operations commander said the victim was found safe about 20m down the ravine.
The victim said he had been hiking alone since 4pm on Sunday before he slipped and fell into the ravine.
He said he could not contact anyone for help because he lost all his belongings during the fall.
The team used a body harness and a rope system to haul the victim up.
The victim did not suffer any injuries and was in a stable condition.
He was handed over to the police at 11.15pm for further action and to have his statement recorded before being sent to the Sultan Abdul Halim Hospital in Sungai Petani for medical treatment.
The operation ended at 11.36pm.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: Shoppers go bananas over monkey business at Sungai Petani grocery store
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: It was monkey business at a grocery store in Bandar Puteri Jaya here after a primate wandered into the premises, as shoppers and workers went "bananas".
The monkey was seen climbing a beam near the ceiling, turning an otherwise routine grocery run into a frenzied scene.
In a video posted by Instagram user @madhilmie_, shoppers and workers were seen reacting frantically as the monkey perched above them.
He wrote that the monkey's arrival caused chaos at the grocery store, claiming that the primate had previously stolen bread elsewhere and had now "returned for groceries".
The post quickly drew reactions from social media users, many amused by the unexpected supermarket sweep.
User @ayampenyetadilia said the monkey caused a stir while people were doing their morning shopping.
"We were buying groceries early in the morning and it already caused chaos. It was huge. People were scared to go in because it was aggressive," the user wrote, while posting an image of the monkey.
Another user, @clara.duvant, suggested that habitat loss may have driven the monkey into the area.
"That's what happens when more trees are cut down there until the monkeys have nowhere to find food," the user wrote.
User @aishah_ahmad_ani joked: "It must have been bored living in the forest."
The poster later shared that the monkey had left the store and was seen loitering at the parking lot, where it was rummaging through rubbish.
The New Straits Times has reached out to the Wildlife and National Parks Department for comment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: South Korea exports post strongest growth since 1978
Article:
SEOUL: South Korea's exports expanded at the strongest pace in nearly half a century last month, smashing forecasts, driven by a surge in chip sales fuelled by the global boom in AI investment.
Exports from Asia's fourth-largest economy rose 70.9 per cent in June from a year earlier to US$102.25 billion, accelerating from the 53.4 per cent increase in May and marking the biggest year-on-year rise since October 1978, preliminary trade data showed on Wednesday.
The annual growth rate exceeded the median forecast of 61.0 per cent in a Reuters poll, surpassing all 13 projections provided by economists.
Semiconductor exports surged 199.5 per cent to US$44.8 billion, making South Korea the fourth country in the world to reach a monthly export value of US$100 billion, after Germany, China and the US, according to the trade ministry.
Computer exports also rose 308.8 per cent on increasing AI investment by major technology firms, while steel product exports snapped 13 consecutive months of decline to rise 9.6 per cent, supported by data centre construction. Petroleum product exports increased 49.8 per cent on higher oil prices.
By destination, shipments to China and the US rose 92.1 per cent and 78.6 per cent, respectively, while exports to the European Union increased 31.8 per cent. Exports to the Middle East fell 8.4 per cent.
Imports rose 30.1 per cent to US$66.10 billion, after increasing 20.7 per cent in the previous month. That was faster than the 26.3 per cent increase expected by economists and the strongest growth since May 2022.
The country posted a monthly trade surplus of US$36.15 billion, the largest on record.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Middle East conflict drags global air travel demand lower in May, IATA says
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Global air passenger demand fell for the first time in more than a year in May as the conflict in the Middle East continued to disrupt international travel, although underlying demand remained resilient in most regions, said the International Air Transport Association (IATA).
IATA said total passenger demand, measured in revenue passenger kilometres (RPK), declined 2.2 per cent year-on-year (YoY) in May 2026, while available seat kilometres (ASK) fell 2.3 per cent. Despite the slowdown, the global load factor edged up to a record 83.5 per cent for the month, indicating airlines continued to fill a high proportion of available seats.
Excluding the Middle East, however, global passenger demand grew 0.7 per cent, underscoring the region's outsized impact on industry performance.
International passenger traffic slipped 1.6 per cent from a year earlier, while capacity contracted 2.4 per cent. The international load factor rose 0.7 percentage point to 83.7 per cent.
Domestic travel was weaker, with demand falling 3.1 per cent and capacity down 2.1 per cent, resulting in a load factor of 83 per cent.
IATA Director General Willie Walsh said the overall decline was largely driven by airlines in the Middle East, where passenger demand plunged 28.4 per cent YoY.
"That's a significant improvement on the 46.6 per cent decline recorded for April, a sign of the region's resilience. Notably, we also saw YoY contractions in demand in both North America and Asia, largely related to domestic market conditions in the US and China," he said.
Overall, Walsh said May demand still appeared to be largely resilient in the face of high fuel prices and air fares.
He said while the recent sharp drop in oil prices is an encouraging development, the challenges created by the war will likely persist for some time.
Oil supply through the Strait of Hormuz remains uncertain, and it is likely to take time before the benefit of lower oil prices is reflected in 'normalised' jet fuel pricing.
"In the meantime, airlines that are operating on a 2.0 per cent margin will have little choice but to continue testing demand resilience with higher fares that attempt to cover elevated fuel costs," he said.
By region, Europe posted the strongest growth among major international markets, with passenger demand rising 3.8 per cent and load factors reaching 85.4 per cent. IATA attributed part of the growth to a 15 per cent increase in direct traffic between Europe and Asia.
Asia-Pacific carriers recorded a modest 1.3 per cent increase in international demand despite capacity cuts caused by tighter jet fuel import restrictions in Vietnam, which affected short-haul intra-Asia services. The region posted a load factor of 85.3 per cent.
North American airlines reported a one per cent increase in international demand, with load factors improving to 84 per cent.
The Middle East remained the weakest-performing region, with demand declining 28.8 per cent, capacity down 24.3 per cent and load factors falling to 76.1 per cent as the effects of the Iran conflict continued to weigh on travel. However, IATA said the pace of decline had nearly halved from April, suggesting conditions were gradually stabilising.
Latin America delivered the strongest growth globally, with passenger demand rising 10.5 per cent, followed by Africa, where demand increased 8.9 per cent.
IATA represents more than 370 airlines, accounting for about 85 per cent of global air traffic.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump earned over US$1bil from crypto ventures in 2025
Article:
NEW YORK: President Donald Trump recorded around US$1.2 billion in income from his cryptocurrency activities in 2025, according to documents released Tuesday by the US Office of Government Ethics.
A 1978 law requires the president and vice president of the United States to declare their income as well as their assets.
According to the documents, which are more than 900 pages long, Trump received nearly US$550 million from his ties to the startup World Liberty Financial.
The Trump family lent its support and its name to this cryptocurrency platform, launched in September 2024.
World Liberty Financial issued its own cryptocurrency, WLFI, whose initial sale brought in US$550 million.
Trump and his three sons also obtained, via an intermediary company, DT Marks Defi, an additional 22.5 billion WLFI, currently worth around US$1.3 billion.
In April 2025, WLF also marketed its stablecoin – a digital currency whose value is pegged to a traditional currency, in this case the dollar.
Trump's income disclosure also mentions US$635 million in royalties received under a licensing agreement related to the $TRUMP cryptocurrency, launched just hours before his inauguration in January 2025.
The president's activities in the cryptocurrency sector are the main reason for the near tripling of his personal fortune, which rose from US$2.3 billion to US$6.5 billion between 2024 and 2026, according to Forbes.
The former real estate developer is regularly accused of conflicts of interest, in particular for having invested in the crypto-currency industry while as president taking several measures to deregulate the sector, causing asset prices to soar.
Beyond the income derived from WLF and its cryptocurrency, Trump has also earned several million dollars from shares in various publicly listed companies active in cryptocurrencies, such as the Coinbase exchange platform.
The president's assets are held in a trust managed by his son, Donald Trump Jr. But its bylaws stipulate that the entity can be dissolved at any time, which means the billionaire could regain control of it as soon as his second term ends.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nike flags prolonged turnaround as China slump, weak outlook eclipse quarterly beat
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Nike signalled on Tuesday that chief executive officer Elliott Hill's turnaround strategy still faces significant obstacles, as persistent weakness in China and a cautious outlook overshadowed a modest fourth-quarter revenue beat.
Quarterly results, which include a 1.0 per cent decline in revenue and a double-digit sales decline in China, failed to reassure investors seeking clear signs that Hill's nearly two-year effort to revive growth is gaining traction.
The sportswear giant also projected a further revenue drop through the first half of fiscal 2027, as it continues to grapple with stiff competition and elevated inventory levels that have hindered its recovery.
"Overall, the results aren't there yet," Hill said on a post-earnings call, expressing dismay at the turnaround timeline for the second consecutive quarter. "We know we're not living up to our full potential."
Nike's shares have fallen 35 per cent so far this year. They fell roughly 4.0 per cent in extended trading on Tuesday before recouping some of the losses.
While Hill, who took the helm in 2024, said efforts to reset Nike's strategy by refocusing on sports, rebuilding wholesale relationships and introducing more products are still inconsistent, he acknowledged on an analyst call that progress "continues to be uneven".
Nike plans to launch more than a dozen footwear styles, he said, adding that it will take time for those products to deliver consistent results.
"Our consumer is under pressure around the world, and we can particularly see it having a larger impact on sportswear," outgoing finance chief Matthew Friend said.
The operating environment became more challenging through the fourth quarter and was not expected to improve over the next six months, Friend said, adding that tariffs remain a continuing cost headwind.
Nike's turnaround has been complicated by tariffs, geopolitical uncertainty and consumer caution, as well as internal efforts to clear older lifestyle-focused inventory that have weighed on margins.
"Expectations were low, and Nike had a sales decline, so these are still not good results," Morningstar analyst David Swartz said.
TROUBLES IN CHINA
China remains a key source of concern for Nike. Sales in Greater China fell 17 per cent on a constant-currency basis in the fourth quarter, steepening from a 10 per cent decline in the previous quarter.
While the drop was slightly less severe than the 20 per cent decline Nike had projected three months earlier, the region continues to suffer with weak product assortments and market-share losses to domestic rivals such as Anta and Li Ning.
China revenue trends were expected to remain broadly in line with recent steep declines as Nike continues working with retail partners to clean up excess inventory in the market, Friend said.
Greater China accounts for about 15 per cent of Nike's annual revenue and is its third-largest market after North America and Europe, the Middle East and Africa.
Despite the challenges, Nike pointed to early signs of progress.
As part of efforts to reignite growth, the company has stepped up marketing around this year's World Cup and accelerated product launches to counter competitors including Adidas.
The company said demand is improving for its football products after a temporary slowdown in April and forecast the first-quarter gross margin to be slightly positive.
Revenue in North America, its largest market, rose 3.0 per cent in the fourth quarter, helping boost wholesale sales as Nike benefits from renewed efforts to rebuild relationships with retailers that had been sidelined under former CEO John Donahoe's direct-to-consumer strategy.
The company reported earnings per share of 72 cents for the quarter, aided by a 52-cent boost tied to the anticipated recovery of import tariffs.
Nike reported a US$986 million benefit from tariff refunds for the three-month period ended May 31. The company had previously projected last October that tariffs would cost it around US$1.5 billion overall.
On an adjusted basis, the company reported quarterly earnings per share of 20 cents, beating estimates of 13 cents, according to data compiled by LSEG.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Household debt stands at RM1.73 trillion, 84.4pct of GDP, Anwar says
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's household debt stood at RM1.73 trillion at the end of March 2026, equivalent to 84.4 per cent of gross domestic product (GDP), Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
Anwar, who is also finance minister, said the ratio had improved slightly from 84.7 per cent at the end of December last year.
He said the government, with the support of Bank Negara Malaysia, continuously monitors household indebtedness, loan repayment capacity and broader economic developments to ensure Malaysians can withstand potential global economic shocks.
"The impaired household loan ratio remained stable and low at one per cent at the end of December 2025, compared with 1.1 per cent in June 2025," he said in a written reply to Aminolhuda Hassan (PH-Sri Gading), who asked how the government was assessing Malaysians' financial resilience in the face of a possible global recession.
Anwar said indicators of household repayment capacity remained strong, with the median debt service ratio (DSR) for outstanding household loans standing at 33 per cent.
He added that debt growth continued to be supported by income growth, as reflected in the stable median debt-to-income ratio of 1.3 times.
"Overall, households continue to have sufficient capacity to meet their debt obligations," he said.
Anwar said the government remained committed to ensuring household debt growth remained under control and in line with borrowers' repayment capacity.
To ease financial pressures arising from the conflict in the Middle East, he said the banking system continued to provide targeted repayment assistance to affected borrowers, including loan restructuring, rescheduling and temporary repayment holidays.
Anwar said the targeted approach was consistent with Bank Negara Malaysia's assessment that the economic impact of the conflict varied across borrowers, ensuring assistance reached those most in need while safeguarding financial system stability.
Affected individuals and businesses are encouraged to engage with their respective banks as early as possible to discuss repayment solutions suited to their financial circumstances, he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit opens firmer against greenback as US posts weaker consumer confidence data
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit was firmer against the US dollar in the early session, supported by weaker-than-expected US consumer confidence data that weighed on the greenback.
Meanwhile, investors remained cautious ahead of the release of the US nonfarm payrolls report later this week.
At 8.01am, the local currency rose to 4.0775/0870 against the greenback from Tuesday's close of 4.0825/0870
Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the US Conference Board Consumer Confidence Index came in at 91.2 points in June, well below the consensus estimates of 94.4 points.
"Today, expect the ringgit to remain cautious ahead of the nonfarm payrolls report tomorrow night, with consensus pegging at 114,000 in June versus 172,000 in May.
"It appears market sentiments are focusing on the US Federal Reserve's decision (on a rate hike), and they seem to be ignoring the prevailing crude oil prices where West Texas Intermediate and Brent crude currently hover around US$69.50 per barrel and US$72.92 per barrel," Mohd Afzanizam told Bernama.
At the opening, the ringgit traded mostly higher against a basket of major currencies.
It appreciated against the British pound to 5.4027/4153 from 5.4036/4096 at Tuesday's close, eased versus the euro to 4.6537/6645 from 4.6528/6580  and appreciated vis-a-vis the Japanese yen at 2.5066/5126 from 2.5154/5183 yesterday.
The local note traded mixed against regional currencies.
It went up against the Singapore dollar to 3.1499/1577 from 3.1532/1570 at Tuesday's close, was almost flat versus the Indonesian rupiah at 227.7/228.3 from 227.9/228.3 and was flat against the Philippine peso at 6.64/6.67 from 6.65/6.67.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Japan manufacturing extends growth, caps best quarter since Q1 2014, PMI shows
Article:
TOKYO: Japan's manufacturing sector expanded in June, capping its best quarterly performance since early 2014, as new orders grew at their fastest pace in more than two years, a business survey showed on Wednesday.
The S&P Global Japan Manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) rose to 54.8 in June from 54.5 in May, expanding for a sixth consecutive month and broadly in line with the flash reading.
The 50-point mark separates expansion from contraction.
The latest PMI survey suggests that Japanese manufacturers enjoyed the strongest quarterly performance in over 12 years in June.
Factories continued to expand their production levels at a solid pace, spurred by another marked improvement in customer demand," said Annabel Fiddes, Economics Associate Director at S&P Global Market Intelligence.
Output rose at the second-fastest pace since January 2022, after April this year. Intermediate and investment goods producers increased production, while output among consumer goods manufacturers fell for the first time in three months.
New orders increased at the fastest pace since January 2022, supported by stronger domestic demand and stockpiling by clients seeking to guard against shortages and price increases arising from the Middle East conflict. Export orders also rose solidly.
Employment grew for a 19th consecutive month, with the pace of hiring the joint-fastest since April 2018. Even so, backlogs of work increased for a sixth consecutive month at the joint-steepest rate since February 2014.
Price pressures remained elevated as supplier shortages and shipping delays lengthened lead times. Input cost inflation was unchanged from May at the joint-fastest pace since September 2022, while selling price inflation eased only slightly from May's multi-year high.
Manufacturers remained optimistic that output would rise over the next year, citing demand for AI and semiconductors and higher capital investment.
However, sentiment remained below its historical trend amid concerns over the US-Israeli war with Iran, costs, labour shortages and the weak yen.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Starmer boosts budget to modernise UK military before exit
Article:
MAIDENHEAD, United Kingdom: Britain plans to spend almost £300 billion ($397 billion) over the next four years to modernise its armed forces to counter rising threats, outgoing Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced Tuesday.
More drones, uncrewed vehicles and an upgrade to the UK's nuclear deterrent form part of Starmer's long-awaited 10-year Defence Investment Plan (DIP), which he described as his "legacy."
The proposals see an extra £15 billion being pumped into defence spending up to 2030, the year by which UK intelligence has suggested Russia could attack a NATO country.
Starmer called the £300 billion figure a "record investment" that would transform Britain's military, as the nature of modern warfare changes.
"By any measure, this is a huge historic shift for our nation and a legacy in which I take pride," he told reporters.
Starmer is expected to leave office next month after losing the support of Labour members of parliament and urged likely successor Andy Burnham to see through his defence spending commitment.
"I am absolutely certain that this is the platform on which whoever comes after me can build," Starmer added.
The plan follows months of wrangling within the Labour government over the resources required to modernise Britain's fighting capabilities, to counter greater aggression from countries such as Russia, and mitigate for a less reliable United States.
Two defence ministers quit earlier this month in a row over the defence plan, including defence secretary John Healey who said it risked making Britain "less safe."
The resignations highlighted Starmer's weakening authority.
He announced on June 22 he was stepping down after MPs from his ruling Labour party switched their support to veteran politician Burnham.
Burnham, the 56-year-old former Greater Manchester mayor, will take over as prime minister in mid-July if he faces no challengers for the Labour leadership.
- 'Hybrid' vessels -
Starmer's plan includes more than £5.0 billion for drones and autonomous systems over the next four years, the Ministry of Defence (MoD) said in a press release.
The investment will see capabilities ranging from "highly complex autonomous mine-hunting drones to small 'quadcopter' tactical drones, and low-cost 'kamikaze' one-way attack drones", the MoD added.
The wars in Ukraine and Iran have highlighted the increased use of war-fighting robots.
Ukraine uses roughly 200,000 drones a month to defend itself from Russia's invasion, while at the height of the US-Israel-Iran conflict 700 offensive drones were being launched per day, the MoD said.
The plan seeks to develop a "hybrid" Royal Navy by replacing six ageing destroyer warships with smaller, autonomous vessels working alongside crewed ships.
It also allocates more than £8 billion to create the next generation of stealth fighter jet alongside Italy and Japan, and a $64 billion upgrade to Britain's nuclear deterrent.
Starmer, who had already cut the international aid budget to boost defence spending, said cuts to road and energy projects would help pay for the new uplift.
US President Donald Trump has demanded that NATO members spend five percent of economic growth on defence by 2035 and become less reliant on Washington for security.
Starmer said the defence plan will take defence spending to 4.2 percent of gross domestic product, but did not specify a date.
The £15 billion uplift is well short of the £28 billion that military leaders previously said was needed.
NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte welcomed Starmer's plan.
"Stronger UK defence makes us all safer," he wrote on X.
But the opposition Conservatives' defence spokesperson, James Cartlidge, said the plan was "too little, too late."
"The plan is now almost a year overdue and only being rushed through because Keir Starmer is desperate for a legacy," he added.
Bence Nemeth, a defence studies lecturer at King's College London, said that while the announcement was "an improvement" it was "clearly not enough", noting that most of the money was going to major projects already in the pipeline rather than new capabilities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: South Korea factory activity grows at slower pace as export demand weakens, PMI shows
Article:
SEOUL: South Korea's factory activity expanded in June for the seventh consecutive month, though at a slower pace than the previous month amid weakening export demand, a survey showed on Wednesday.
The purchasing managers' index (PMI), published by S&P Global, stood at 52.1 in June, down from May's 54.8, which was the highest level in more than five years.
Output and new orders grew more slowly, while new export orders fell for the second consecutive month due to the lingering impact of the Middle East conflict, according to the survey.
"Firms frequently reported that rising raw material prices, alongside difficulties sourcing and receiving inputs due to delays and shortages, weighed on sector performance," said Usamah Bhatti, economist at S&P Global Market Intelligence.
Business optimism for the year ahead weakened to its lowest level since November 2025 amid concerns over the health of the domestic economy and the potential for a prolonged period of high raw material prices.
South Korea's factory output fell for the second consecutive month in May, missing expectations, data showed on Tuesday. The economy grew in the first quarter at the strongest pace in nearly six years, driven by a boom in chip exports.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: World Bank to phase out lending to China by 2031
Article:
WASHINGTON: The World Bank will phase out its lending to China by 2031, according to the organisation's new country partnership framework, a source familiar with the matter told AFP on Tuesday.
The source confirmed an earlier report by the Financial Times.
"China has made significant development advances over the past several decades, progress that the World Bank and others have supported," said a World Bank official familiar with the matter, speaking on condition of anonymity.
"Now we are reaching a new phase of our relationship, reflecting that reality."
World Bank lending to China, the world's second-largest economy, has steadily declined in recent years as the Asian giant experienced rapid growth and a reduction in poverty indicators.
In his first term in office, US President Donald Trump demanded that the World Bank stop lending to China entirely, as he adopted a more aggressive approach towards Washington's chief economic rival.
Trump has maintained that tone in his second term but has not specifically repeated that demand.
World Bank lending to China peaked at US$2.42 billion in 2017 but has fallen since then, declining to US$750 million in 2025.
China also contributes funds to the World Bank's International Development Association (IDA) pool for the world's least developed countries, with its US$1.5 billion contribution under the latest replenishment round making Beijing the fifth-largest donor.
"The World Bank's role is shifting from lender to knowledge partner, in line with China's development trajectory," said the World Bank official.
On June 16, the World Bank announced a similar plan for Poland, aiming to reduce lending to zero by 2031 while maintaining technical assistance.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Missing boy, 6, found dead after search near Perak oil palm plantation
Article:
BAGAN DATUK: A six-year-old boy reported missing from his home in Sungai Sumun, Hutan Melintang, was found dead in a drain at a nearby oil palm plantation last night.
Muhammad Faith Fatih Abdullah Fattah's body was found by members of the public at 10.08pm, about 330m from his home, following a search and rescue operation launched earlier in the evening.
Perak Fire and Rescue Department operations division assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the department received a call from the police at 6.24pm reporting the child's disappearance.
He said the boy's family discovered he was missing after realising he was not in the house and that the front door had been left open
"The victim's father immediately searched the area around the house and the nearby oil palm plantation but was unable to find him.
"He later lodged a police report at 3.30pm reporting his son's disappearance," Sabarodzi said today.
He said the operation involved personnel from the Hutan Melintang Fire and Rescue Station, police, other relevant agencies and members of the public.
He added that a police K9 unit was deployed to assist in the search of the oil palm plantation behind the victim's home.
"The victim was found by members of the public in a drain within the plantation. Firefighters later recovered the body."
"The operation concluded at 11.11pm," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Japan business mood improves despite Middle East war, BOJ survey shows
Article:
TOKYO: Big Japanese manufacturers' sentiment improved in the three months to June to levels unseen since 2018, a closely watched survey showed on Wednesday, a sign the economy was weathering the energy shock from the Middle East conflict for now.
The headline index measuring big manufacturers' business sentiment stood at +22 in June, up from +17 in March, and exceeded a median market forecast of +16. It marked the highest level since March 2018.
An index gauging big non-manufacturers' sentiment stood at +37, up from +36 in March. It beat a median market forecast of +35 to mark the highest level since August 1991.
The outcome will be among the factors the BOJ will scrutinise at its next policy meeting on **July 30 to 31**.
The BOJ raised interest rates to a 31-year high in June in a landmark step towards policy normalisation, signalling its readiness to tighten further as it focuses on containing price pressures from the Iran war-induced energy shock.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three-year-old rescued six days after Venezuela quakes
Article:
CARACAS: A three-year-old boy was rescued from the rubble in Caracas on Tuesday, a Jordanian rescue team said, six days after twin earthquakes hit Venezuela, killing almost 2,000 people.
Video footage showed rescue workers cheering upon discovering the child, who had miraculously survived.
The boy received first aid and was immediately taken to hospital, the Jordanian civil defense said in a statement.
In other footage, rescue workers wiped the boy's face with tissues and tucked him in with a blanket in an ambulance.
Experts say the 72-hour window after an earthquake serves as the crucial window during which people trapped under debris have a chance of being rescued alive.
The infant's vital signs were good, the Jordanian statement said, adding that local authorities had been informed of the rescue.
Last week's 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude shocks – one of the worst earthquake disasters in Latin America – collapsed whole residential complexes, left tens of thousands unaccounted for and prompted frantic search-and-rescue operations for survivors trapped in the rubble.
Entire buildings were pulverized in La Guaira, an area north of the capital Caracas, which also endured severe damage.
Venezuelan parliamentary president Jorge Rodriguez said Tuesday that 6,461 people had been rescued since the quake struck.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Easier, greener bill management system
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR As Malaysians become increasingly connected through digital technology, many daily tasks that once required paperwork can now be completed with just a few taps on a smartphone.
Banking, shopping and communication have largely moved online. Yet many households still rely on traditional paper bills for utilities.
One simple change can make life easier while supporting a more sustainable future: switching to Air Selangor's e-Bil for easier water bill management.
For Air Selangor customers, e-Bil offers a convenient, efficient, and environmental-friendly way to manage water accounts.
Instead of waiting for a physical bill to arrive each month, customers can check their billing information instantly through the Air Selangor app or online platform anytime, anywhere.
Important account information is always accessible, eliminating the need to keep stacks of old bills or search through paperwork.
The ability to make payments digitally also saves time.
Rather than travelling to a payment counter or searching for a physical bill to make a transaction, customers can settle their accounts quickly and securely through their mobile devices.
Beyond convenience, switching to e-Bil contributes to environmental sustainability.
[[nid:1477350]]
Every paper bill requires resources to produce, print and distribute.
Although a single bill may seem insignificant, the environmental impact becomes substantial when multiplied across millions of households.
By choosing e-Bil, customers help reduce paper consumption and support efforts to minimise waste.
The Air Selangor app also offers customers greater control over their water usage.
Among the features available, they can monitor their monthly water consumption with detailed information.
This helps households better understand their consumption patterns, identify unusual spikes in usage and manage monthly expenses effectively.
When families can clearly see how much water they are using, they become more conscious of wastage and internal leaks. This not only helps reduce expenditure, but also encourages responsible water consumption.
The digital platform further enhances customer service by providing access to important updates and support services.
Users can receive real-time notifications about water supply disruptions, maintenance work and other service announcements.
The app also simplifies the reporting process for leaking pipes, burst mains or faulty meters, leading to quicker action in reducing water loss and improving overall service efficiency.
For customers who require assistance, Air Selangor's digital ecosystem includes the chatbot Joy, which provides round-the-clock support for common enquiries.
As Malaysia continues its digital transformation journey, adopting services such as e-Bil reflects a broader shift towards smarter and efficient living.
It allows households to embrace technology while contributing to environmental conservation and resource management.
The transition is simple, but the benefits are significant. Customers enjoy greater convenience, improved account management and easier access to information, while helping reduce paper waste and supporting sustainability efforts.
In an increasingly digital world, receiving and paying water bills electronically is no longer just an option — it is a smarter way forward.
By registering for e-Bil through the Air Selangor app or website, customers can take a small but meaningful step towards a convenient, efficient and sustainable future.
Download the Air Selangor app via the App Store, Google Play or Huawei AppGallery and register for e-Bil today.
[[nid:1477348]]
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Businesses back MTEN efforts, but seek greater policy detail
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Business groups welcome government efforts to cushion geopolitical impacts but say lack of details and engagement limits assessment of effectiveness.
They cited high costs, weak demand and cash flow pressures, while calling for financing, digitalisation support and regulatory relief.
The SME Association of Malaysia national president Dr Chin Chee Seong welcomed the government's proactive approach but said it had yet to receive details on the more than 120 measures approved under the National Economic Action Council (MTEN).
"As we have not been briefed on the details of these measures, it would be premature for us to assess whether they are adequate," he told Business Times.
This comes after Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the government had implemented 27 out of 120 decisions approved by MTEN.
The remaining 93 are currently being rolled out to cushion the impact of recurring global economic disruptions arising from the conflict in the Middle East.
Chin said feedback from its members indicated that businesses continue to grapple with rising operating costs, subdued domestic demand, cash flow constraints, labour shortages and global market uncertainty.
"If some of the MTEN measures are already producing positive results, that is certainly encouraging.
"However, from the perspective of many SMEs, the overall business environment remains challenging, and more time may be needed before businesses can experience tangible improvements on the ground," he added.
Chin urged the government to strengthen engagement with industry players so businesses can better understand the initiatives and provide feedback based on actual industry needs.
He noted that improving SME cash flow and reducing the cost of doing business should remain the government's top policy priority.
"Many SMEs are not asking for permanent assistance. What they need is support that enables them to remain competitive during this period of uncertainty.
"This includes easier access to financing, incentives for digitalisation and automation, measures to ease regulatory compliance, and avoiding the introduction of additional business costs while economic conditions remain fragile," he said.
The Small and Medium Enterprises Association (Samenta) national president Datuk William Ng said the association had met Economy Ministry officials twice on the issue
He welcomed the government's transparency and urgency in responding to the evolving crisis.
"Malaysia has benefitted tremendously as a trusted partner during this conflict and crisis. If we are able to tap rapidly into these shifting trade rules, we will stand out even more compared to the rest of Asean," he said.
He, however, cautioned that the geopolitical supply chain disruptions, particularly those affecting logistics, energy, fertiliser and raw material costs, are expected to persist for at least another 18 months.
Ng said while measures such as the RM5 billion liquidity facilities and financing guarantees had provided a critical short-term cushion, businesses were increasingly being squeezed by structural cost pressures.
"From the ground perspective of exporting and manufacturing SMEs, the relief is being rapidly outpaced by structural macro pressures.
"While physical supply lines are being kept open, SMEs are battling rising domestic energy costs, labour costs and an expected increase in imported input costs. The financial cushion helps, but it acts as a temporary bandage."
Ng also said exporters are facing growing compliance requirements from international buyers, who now demand end-to-end transparency across supply chains amid heightened scrutiny over forced labour and changing global trade rules.
He proposed matching grants for supply chain digitalisation to help SMEs invest in traceability systems and meet international standards.
"Most tier-two and tier-three SMEs simply do not have the IT infrastructure or capital to track global supply chains to that level. We are hopeful that the government will consider providing SMEs with matching grants for supply chain digitalisation.
"This will transform this crisis into an opportunity; allowing Malaysia to move up the value chain and position our SME ecosystem as 'new supply chain ready'," he added.
Several chambers of commerce and industry associations contacted by Business Times declined to comment on the effectiveness of the measures, saying they had not received a detailed briefing from the government on the initiatives.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: LiveWire fuels startup growth beyond funding
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: For many startups, securing funding is only one part of the equation.
Building credibility, gaining access to industry networks and finding experienced mentors can be just as critical in turning a promising idea into a sustainable business.
These are among the advantages offered by Shell LiveWire Malaysia, which returns for its 2026 edition with a continued focus on helping entrepreneurs refine their business models, strengthen their ventures and accelerate growth.
Among its success stories is PlusVibes founder and chief executive officer Dr Madiha Fuad, who credits the programme with accelerating the company's expansion after emerging as the Peninsular Malaysia winner in 2024.
Founded in 2020, PlusVibes is Malaysia's first on-demand mental health platform connecting users with therapists anonymously while providing digital well-being tools, corporate mental health programmes and community initiatives.
Since its establishment, the social enterprise has supported some 90,000 individuals across the country.
For Madiha, the business was born from both personal experience and the growing need for accessible mental healthcare.
"Over the years, I met many individuals who were struggling emotionally but did not know where to seek help. Some felt ashamed, some could not afford support, and others simply did not have access to the right resources.
"A major turning point in my life came when I experienced a health incident that left me semi-paralysed for a year. During my recovery journey, I realised how critical emotional support is when facing life's challenges. Healing is not just physical, it is also emotional," she said.
While building the business, Madiha came across Shell LiveWire through Malaysia's startup ecosystem.
"What particularly stood out was the credibility that comes with being associated with Shell.
As entrepreneurs, especially in the early stages of building a business, credibility is incredibly important.
"Having the support of a globally respected organisation like Shell gives founders confidence, opens doors to meaningful opportunities, and helps build trust with potential partners, customers and stakeholders," she said.
Madiha said winning the 2024 competition marked a significant milestone in her entrepreneurial journey, one she remains deeply grateful for.
FROM RECOGNITION TO GROWTH
Since winning Shell LiveWire, PlusVibes has continued to expand its reach and capabilities.
Madiha said it opened doors to potential partners, investors, clients and other stakeholders who might not have otherwise discovered PlusVibes.
"It also gave us access to a valuable network of entrepreneurs and mentors who generously shared their experiences, insights and guidance.
"In fact, I still keep in touch with many of the mentors, fellow entrepreneurs and connections I made through the programme, which speaks volumes about the strength of the LiveWire community.
"The visibility we gained through Shell LiveWire also played an important role in expanding our reach. This year, PlusVibes has been able to significantly grow our corporate services and work with more organisations across various industries," she said.
Madiha said PlusVibes has recorded significant growth since winning Shell LiveWire, expanding its services, strengthening its technology platform, and forging partnerships across the corporate, education and community sectors.
"We have also grown our network of mental health professionals and support providers. Most importantly, we have been able to support thousands more individuals on their mental health journeys, which remains our greatest measure of success," she said.
Madiha said the programme also helped her develop a more strategic mindset in building a sustainable business.
She said one of the most valuable lessons she gained was that a strong idea is only the starting point of creating a successful venture.
"It challenged me to refine my business model, strengthen my value proposition, understand my customers better and communicate my vision with greater confidence.
"More importantly, it taught me resilience. Entrepreneurship is rarely a straight path, and success often comes from the ability to learn, adapt and keep moving forward despite challenges," Madiha said.
REGISTRATIONS OPEN
For entrepreneurs hoping to follow a similar path, Shell LiveWire Malaysia 2026 is now accepting registrations.
Established in 2015, the programme equips participants through a series of workshops covering business model development, idea validation and pitching before culminating in a competition.
Five winners each from Peninsular Malaysia, Sabah and Sarawak will receive RM10,000 in seed funding before competing at the national level, where five winners will stand a chance to receive an additional RM30,000.
Successful participants will also receive mentorship for up to three years and opportunities to explore collaborations within Shell's supply chain and retail network.
Applications for Peninsular Malaysia close on July 6.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Angkasa fast tracks RM200mil Boost stake buy: Sources
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Angkatan Koperasi Kebangsaan Malaysia Bhd (Angkasa) is accelerating plans to buy a RM200 million strategic stake in Boost Holdings Sdn Bhd.
Sources said the cooperative aims to complete the transaction within the next few weeks after making an initial payment of RM30 million.
Boost is the largest shareholder of Boost Bank Bhd, one of the five digital banks licensed by Bank Negara Malaysia.
The investment would give Angkasa a direct entry into the country's fast-growing digital banking sector.
Sources said the proposed investment had secured approvals from both Angkasa's delegates at its 40th annual general meeting on June 27 and Bank Negara.
Documents seen by Business Times showed the investment received the central bank's approval under an Enforceable Undertaking on Oct 1, 2025, subject to specified conditions and regulatory requirements.
The proposal amends an earlier RM200 million mandate approved at Angkasa's 2025 delegates meeting to align with Bank Negara's requirements governing ownership in licensed digital banking institutions.
Boost is reportedly 77.76 per cent-owned by Axiata Group Bhd. Great Eastern Digital Pte Ltd and Mitsuit & Co Ltd hold stakes of 19.9 per cent and 2.34 per cent respectively in the company.
It holds a 60 per cent stake in Boost Bank, while RHB Bank Bhd owns the remaining 40 per cent.
Axiata and Boost reportedly made no secret of the fact that they had been looking for a strategic investor for the latter.
Angkasa, the umbrella body for Malaysia's cooperative sector, manages a salary-deduction system widely used by civil servants to repay cooperative financing.
There are reportedly 17,000 cooperatives under Angkasa, with a membership reaching 7.4 million people.
Based on the documents, Angkasa believes the investment in Boost reflects its efforts to ensure it remains relevant and competitive amid the rapid expansion of the digital economy and financial technology sector.
Digital banking has emerged as a key growth driver by offering faster, more accessible and lower-cost financial services, while cashless payments, digital wallets and online financing have become increasingly embedded in business and consumer activities.
Of the proposed RM200 million investment, RM170 million will be financed through borrowings from financial institutions, while the remaining RM30 million from Angkasa's internal funds.
The financing structure will enable it to undertake a large-scale strategic investment without materially affecting its cash flow and operational requirements, while positioning the cooperative to benefit from long-term growth opportunities in Malaysia's digital financial ecosystem.
The investment will be implemented through MyAngkasa Digital Services Sdn Bhd, which will act as the main vehicle for equity ownership and the cooperative's strategic participation in digital banking.
MyAngkasa Digital is 48.5 per cent-owned by Angkasa, whose stake is expected to increase to 79.13 per cent upon completion of a shareholding restructuring.
The opportunity to own shares in MyAngkasa Digital will subsequently be extended to the cooperative's members through a tokenisation programme, with further details to be announced later.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: NASA rolls out more Moon base plans
Article:
WASHINGTON: NASA on Tuesday announced new uncrewed missions to aid in the future creation of a lunar surface base, a project beginning to take shape despite recent setbacks.
Three US companies were awarded contracts for missions to deliver cargo including scientific instruments to the Moon, said NASA, which will pay nearly $600 million for the projects that supplement others announced in May.
The efforts are part of plans to use robotic vehicles to build infrastructure that future human explorers to the Moon could eventually make use of.
The announcement comes as the US space agency's lunar ambitions have faced setbacks following the spectacular explosion this spring of the New Glenn rocket, which is built by tech billionaire Jeff Bezos's company Blue Origin.
Senior agency officials have acknowledged that the situation would likely cause lunar programme delays, but voiced optimism Tuesday, also indicating there were alternatives to launch a lander carrying equipment developed by Blue Origin.
"We're working with Blue Origin very closely to understand their timelines to recovery and also looking at other options in case it doesn't meet our timeline," said Carlos Garcia-Galan, NASA programme manager for the Moon base, during an event with journalists Tuesday.
After spending years to create a lunar orbital space station known as Gateway, NASA overhauled its plans in March, saying it would be focused now on creating a base for surface operations.
They pledged $20 billion to the Moon base efforts.
The base is planned for a site near the lunar south pole, a strategic area due to the presence of water ice in the soil. NASA had previously said construction could begin in 2029.
Conceived to compete with China – which also aims to send humans to the Moon and establish a base there – NASA's ambitious plan remains vague in several aspects.
But the US agency has started to roll out its vision, which includes lunar landers, lunar rovers and drones designed to survey the terrain and subsequently build up infrastructure including a power grid and pressurised habitats.
The agency on Tuesday floated the possibility of repurposing a Mars rover as part of the efforts they're aiming to complete in a tight timeframe. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Venezuela quake survivors seek food, shelter as toll rises to nearly 2,000
Article:
LA GUAIRA: Tens of thousands of people urgently need food and shelter in Venezuela after the two devastating earthquakes that killed nearly 2,000, the UN said on Tuesday, as doctors warned of outbreaks of disease with survivors sleeping in the streets.
Last week's 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude shocks – one of the worst earthquake disasters in Latin America – collapsed whole residential complexes, left tens of thousands unaccounted for and prompted frantic search-and-rescue operations for survivors trapped in the rubble.
The UN refugee agency said "food shortages are widespread, basic services have broken down and connectivity has been largely severed" in the port city of La Guaira – the worst hit area north of the capital Caracas.
"They give out supplies here, but sometimes people nearly kill each other for food...it's like a cockfight," said Daniela Armas, 18, a vendor in La Guaira who was injured falling from a motorbike during the quakes.
In a daily update, Venezuela's National Assembly President Jorge Rodriguez said deaths had risen to 1,943, with more than 10,500 injured after one of Latin America's worst earthquake disasters.
He said nearly 6,500 people had been rescued from the rubble in La Guaira, but that number was likely closer to 20,000 including those who escaped or were helped out by family.
Many Venezuelans have expressed anger at the government's slow response to the disaster in a country already struggling with decades of economic crisis that has weakened infrastructure and health services.
The UN agency said it needed some $14.85 million to scale up aid and temporary shelter for 30,000 people over six months.
"Community tensions are rising as access to assistance remains constrained," UNHCR spokesperson Carlotta Wolf said.
The quakes likely damaged or destroyed 58,870 buildings, according to a preliminary assessment of satellite data published by NASA.
World Health Organization spokesman Christian Lindmeier said health services in Venezuela were overstretched and under "extreme pressure."
"There's an increased risk now of outbreaks of vaccine-preventable diseases" such as measles and diphtheria, due to low pre-earthquake vaccination coverage, he said.
BARE HANDS
With international rescue teams from the US, Mexico and dozens of other countries scrambling with trained dogs and heavy equipment to dig out survivors, Venezuelans began burying the dead they could find.
Others were frantically searching by hand for missing loved ones in the rubble, but also in hospitals and morgues.
"It's harder not knowing because you ask yourself, 'What do I do? Where do I look for her?" said Rosanna Luna, looking for her sister Soraida in the wreckage of her home.
AFP journalists on Monday saw black body bags containing victims of the quake lined up near a makeshift morgue at the dock in La Guaira, where many people came for news or to identify remains.
[[nid:1477325]]
Darvin Silva, 37, described how he battled to reach his mother, who died under a pillar in a collapsed building.
"The effort it took me to get her out of there with my bare hands, with sledgehammers, with pickaxes... you can't even begin to imagine," he said.
"I hope that I can now offer her the rest she deserves," he said.
The critical 72-hour window during which survivors were still likely to be found closed on Saturday evening.
But rescuers in hard hats and high-visibility vests were still picking through the twisted metal and shattered concrete.
Around 50,000 people are still listed as missing, according to the UN.
BODY BAGS
Some seven million people in Venezuela would be affected by the disaster, the UN has said, with the quakes knocking a $6.7-billion hole in the economy – or six percent of Venezuela's GDP.
A total of 27 countries have mobilized nearly 40 search-and-rescue teams. They include more than 2,000 troops and personnel, along with more than 160 dogs, according to Gianluca Rampolla, the UN coordinator in Venezuela.
The world body is providing 10,000 body bags, though it hopes the final toll will be lower.
At the only public cemetery in Caracas, the two crematory ovens have been working at full capacity.
At the makeshift morgue at La Guaira's port, many are still waiting for the remains of their loved ones who are presumed dead.
"My family is there – I'm told my sister and her children are there, as well as the children of my brother," Wilker Molalla told AFP as he waited to identify the remains.
"There were 11 people in my household," he said. "Only two of us survived because we were at work."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Alleged access of PM's bank data incredibly concerning'
Article:
Your browser does not support the audio element.
AUSTRALIAN Treasurer Jim Chalmers said yesterday a report that two Ernst & Young (EY) employees on temporary assignment at Commonwealth Bank of Australia (CommBank) allegedly accessed Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's personal banking details was "incredibly concerning".
The Australian Financial Review (AFR) newspaper reported yesterday that EY had sacked two employees who were facing criminal charges after they allegedly accessed Albanese's bank details and those of at least one EY partner.
EY declined to comment on the report. Albanese's office did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
A CommBank spokesperson said "it is not appropriate for us to comment on individual contractor matters".
Chalmers said he would not comment on legal processes under way.
"But I think on the face of it any developments of that kind are incredibly concerning, not just in relation to the PM's details but any Australians' details," said Chalmers.
The report comes as EY's peer KPMG faces an audit leak scandal, adding pressure on Big Four accounting firms after their new-business revenue from the Australian federal government fell by almost half last year.
The Australian Federal Police said it had charged two Sydney men on May 6 with allegedly accessing restricted personal banking data belonging to a federal parliamentarian.
A 21-year-old man was charged with unauthorised access to restricted data and publishing or distributing personal data. A 25-year-old man was charged with unauthorised access to restricted data, intending to cause the access and knowing it was unauthorised.
Federal police did not name the lawmaker, but said both men were granted police bail to appear in a court here yesterday.
"As the matter is before the court, no further comment will be made," a federal police spokesman said by email.
The AFR report, citing unidentified sources, said the two men deployed to CommBank would have received a system warning requiring them to confirm they were permitted to access a customer's confidential information.
Once they agreed they were authorised, the system granted access to personal bank details, the report added. — REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: The 8 Johor hot seats to watch after Pas' strategic shift
Article:
JOHOR BARU:  The battle lines of the Johor election saw a slight shift in the past few days after Perikatan Nasional's Pas instructed its members not to vote for Pakatan Harapan in seats the coalition is not contesting.
Political observers said the shift in battle lines, including Pas' decision to deploy its election machinery only in the 11 seats it is contesting, has changed the dynamics in some of the 56 seats up for grabs in the July 11 polls.
LAYANG-LAYANG
Among the seats being closely watched is Layang-Layang, which is seeing a three-cornered fight.
Abdul Mutalip Abd Rahim is defending the seat, but this time under PN instead of Barisan Nasional after he quit Umno to join Bersatu two days before nomination day.
While Mutalip was initially seen as having the upper hand as he last held the seat, his position is now more uncertain as Bersatu candidates will not get support from Pas' election machinery.
BN has fielded MCA's Chua Jian Boon.
BUKIT KEPONG
In the 2022 state election, Johor Bersatu chairman Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal won the seat with a slim majority of 710 votes.
Without Pas's election machinery this election, observers believe Bersatu faces a daunting task in defending the seat, which will see a three-cornered fight.
ENDAU
Before Nomination Day, former Endau assemblyman Alwiyah Talib announced her decision to quit Bersatu and join Umno. BN then fielded her in the seat.
Adding pressure for Bersatu is the four-cornered fight.
PN fielded Pas's Hasnul Hakimi Hussein in the hope of retaining the seat for the coalition.
The fourth contender is Parti Orang Asli Malaysia's Jati Awang.
MACHAP
All eyes are on Machap as Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi is defending the seat. The caretaker menteri besar is in a straight fight with PH's Nur Hafiz Roslan.
In the 2022 state polls, Onn Hafiz won the seat with majority of 6,543 votes, polling 11,029 votes in total. Machap is one of the 14 seats that will see a straight fight between BN and PH.
PUTERI WANGSA
In 2022, Muda won the seat in its electoral debut. Amanah had passed the seat to Muda to contest as the parties had a cooperation pact. Amira Aisya Abdul Aziz, who is now Muda president, won the seat after polling 22,884 votes, defeating five other contenders.
This election, PH has fielded PKR heavyweight and former education minister Dr Maszlee Malik. Amira Aisya is not defending the seat. Muda has fielded Rashifa Aljunied.
Other contenders are BN's Teow Chia Ling, Bersama's Nicholas Paul Vincent and independent candidate Wang Wee Siong.
PASIR RAJA
Pasir Raja is another seat seeing a former minister in the fight.
BN fielded former health minister Datuk Seri Dr Adham Baba.
Dr Adham, 64, was Pasir Raja assemblyman for two terms from 2008 to 2018. He is facing off against PH's Mohd Fakharuddin Moslim and PN's Yuhanita Yunan.
SENGGARANG
PN has fielded former Dewan Rakyat deputy speaker Datuk Rashid Hasnon of Bersatu in a bid to wrest the seat from BN.
BN has fielded Yusla Ismail, who won the seat in 2022 with a majority of 3,912 votes.
Completing the three-cornered contest is PH's Onn Abu Bakar.
MAHKOTA
In the 2024 by-election, BN's Syed Hussein Syed Abdullah won the seat with a majority of 20,648 votes in a straight fight against PN. PH sat out the by-election and threw its support behind BN as a sign of solidarity between parties in the unity federal government.
This election, Syed Hussein will face PH's Dr Ahmad Zuhan Md Zain and Bersama's Abd Hamid Ali in a three-cornered fight.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Security-enhanced passport launched
Article:
Your browser does not support the audio element.
THE new passport being issued from today will have 94 security features that strengthen protections against forgery, alteration and misuse.
Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said yesterday the improvements, nearly double the previous version's 49, included visual, covert and forensic security features.
He said Malaysians could get either a 10-year passport for RM350 or the five-year passport for RM200.
"Initially, the passport will be issued at 14 state offices, including the headquarters. With these enhanced security features, it will be harder to forge it," he added.
Earlier, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim launched the new passport at the Immigration booth in the Dewan Rakyat lobby.
Present were Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, Zakaria and the prime minister's wife, Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail.
Zakaria said currently, 180,000 to 220,000 passports were issued monthly, adding that the number was expected to rise.
However, he stressed that there was no need to rush to replace valid passports.
"Only those with expired passports or applying for new ones should proceed," he said.
The Home Ministry previously said the new passport had major security upgrades and would provide greater convenience and value for Malaysians.
The security technologies are in line with the standards set by the International Civil Aviation Organisation.
The passport's design motifs are inspired by Malaysia's history, cultural heritage and constitutional monarchy.
Among them, the Rajah Brooke's Birdwing butterfly has replaced the hibiscus as the new icon.
The ministry added that the new printing machines could churn out up to 60 passports an hour.
It added that the upgraded passport reflected the government's commitment to ensuring Malaysia's travel document remained secure, modern, reliable and aligned with international best practices.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Perak opens first Islamic high school for Orang Asli
Article:
Your browser does not support the audio element.
SUNGAI SIPUT: The establishment of the first Islamic secondary school for the Orang Asli community marks a significant milestone for indigenous students.
Sultan of Perak Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah said Sekolah Menengah Agama Rakyat Orang Asli Nurul Hidayah in Kampung Kenang here symbolised the state's commitment to ensuring every child had access to quality education as well as the opportunity to build a better future.
